records.summary: CheckerTypeProjector is the TypeChecker projection frontier for durable and canonicalized type shapes, hot members, related types, and synthetic type-system products.
records.rationale: Expression, observation, template, and LSP-like inquiries should reuse one checker projection substrate instead of each producing local type summaries. This record complements the expression evaluator record by pointing at the lower-level shape publication layer. Telemetry showed that declaration-backed TypeChecker/evaluated-value surfaces should converge by checker key and declaration lane while caller-owned expression, binding, diagnostic, and cursor products preserve user-facing source loci. Checker-owned unions/intersections can also converge structurally when their constituents are source-independent. Synthetic expression/template shapes remain a separate convergence policy frontier because their identity is often produced by the runtime-shaped expression site.
guidance: Start here when repeated type checker keys, hot type-member volume, durable type shapes, related indexed/iterated/call/construct types, synthetic type projections, or query-local TypeChecker projection retention appear in telemetry.
Use CheckerExpressionTypeEvaluator for expression semantics, but keep reusable checker shape publication in CheckerTypeProjector.
Checker keys for declared or library-backed generic surfaces must include the projected display/instantiation; declaration site alone is not enough for TypeChecker shape identity.

Do not use expression source as part of declaration-backed TypeChecker type identity just to preserve cursor loci; the caller-owned expression, binding, diagnostic, or cursor product should carry that source.
Keep declarationSourceAddressHandle as the navigation and owner-type repair lane for checker-backed shapes.

Template-requested checker-returned types should keep TypeChecker origin. Use SyntheticTemplateType only when the template/runtime substrate creates the shape rather than asking the TypeChecker for it.
Let checker-owned union/intersection keys converge structurally only when their constituents are source-independent; anonymous authored object/function shapes should remain source-owned.
Keep synthetic expression/template shapes source-owned until a structural synthetic-type identity policy proves it can preserve the lost runtime expression provenance.

If QueryClaimGraph disposal removes a query-local shape, evict stale projector index rows before returning a reused handle. `contract:type-projection-lifetime` proves the current type-shape index prunes and reprojects after marker disposal.
If type projection cost rises, treat it as inquiry-algebra or caching pressure before duplicating narrow type summaries.

anchors.summary: TypeChecker shape and member projection class.
Chooses whether projection source participates in type-shape identity.
Type-shape convergence and source-lane watchpoints.
Reports repeated type checker keys, hot details, and phase/kernel growth.
Contract proving type-shape sidecar indexes mirror kernel product-detail disposal.

records.summary: CompiledTemplateMaterializer is the current pressure point for template lowering, render-target publication, and controller-facing products.
records.rationale: Template features often look like vertical slice work, but the durable shape should remain grounded in Aurelia compiler, rendering, controller, and binding runtime concepts. The factory and traversal companions in the same module are part of this materialization frontier rather than separate untracked work.
guidance: Before adding a new template product, inspect whether framework.compiler, framework.rendering, or framework.observation already names the runtime concept.

Treat CompiledTemplateInstructionFactory and CompiledTemplateInstructionTraversal as companions of the compiled-template materializer unless framework grounding suggests a cleaner Aurelia-shaped split.
If recursive rendering or controller semantics are involved, keep the controller and view-factory materializers in the loop.

Root `<template>` surrogate failures, `<slot>` shadow-DOM failures, and local-template shape failures that match Aurelia template-compiler throws publish compiler issues in this phase; do not reintroduce generic open seams for those framework-rejected cases.

Custom-element child content projection is a compiler DOM transform: HydrateElementInstruction carries default/named projection instruction sequences, direct parent rows skip extracted children, and `compiler.open-content-projection` should not appear for ordinary slot extraction.
Avoid ad hoc template feature flags when the framework has a renderer, binding, or controller shape for the behavior.

Use routed app-profile phase kernel summaries before optimizing template bulk. Disposed-app answers can now report which template/runtime phases created answer-local records and product details before QueryClaimGraph reclaims them.

anchors.summary: Compiled template materialization class.
Instruction factory companion for compiled template materialization.
Instruction traversal companion for compiled template materialization.
Compiled-template assembly work frame.
Slot-name plus instruction-sequence handle carried by HydrateElementInstruction projections.
Contract that proves custom-element child content lowers into projection sequences without compiler.open-content-projection seams.
Framework rendering graph to compare before product modeling.
Framework compiler instruction-production graph.

records.summary: TemplateCompilerWorldMaterializer is the template compiler-world frontier where resources, syntax, renderers, and configured catalogs become spendable to compilation.
records.rationale: Template work often starts from a binding or renderer symptom, but many correct answers depend on the compiler world that makes resources and syntax visible. Keep this handle durable so future work starts from world construction before local template shortcuts.
guidance: Inspect compiler-world materialization before adding resource, syntax, renderer, or template-authoring world behavior.
Check framework.compiler, framework.resources, and framework.rendering before deciding a compiler-world product is missing.

Modeled template-compiler failures should publish exact framework error authority as `TemplateCompilerIssue` products. Attribute classification owns reserved spread and `$bindables` failures, binding-command lowering owns command build failures, compiled-template assembly owns root surrogate, projection, slot, let, and local-template shape failures, and diagnostics should read those products instead of adding API-local wording checks.
If a template inquiry does not need the full compiler world, treat that as inquiry-algebra pressure rather than duplicating partial state.

anchors.summary: Template compiler-world materializer.
Framework compiler grounding lane.
Framework resource convergence lane.

records.summary: BuiltInSyntaxCatalogMaterializer and its configured companion are the framework-owned syntax catalog frontier before compiler-world visibility.
records.rationale: Aurelia built-in binding commands, attribute patterns, template controllers, i18n/state syntax, and configured framework registrations become compiler-visible through this catalog substrate. Future binding, forms, interpolation, or authoring work should start here before inventing local syntax availability rules.
guidance: Inspect built-in syntax catalog materialization before adding framework-owned binding commands, attribute patterns, or package-specific syntax availability.
Keep raw catalog product materialization distinct from configured catalog selection; the configured companion is where registration/configuration pressure belongs.
Use framework.compiler, framework.resources, and framework configuration pressure before deciding a built-in syntax entry is missing or in the wrong package.

`parseBuiltInAttributeSyntax(...)` is the product-free parser for validating generated/source-lowering attributes against the built-in pattern inventory. Do not confuse it with configured compiler-world parsing; app-specific visibility still flows through compiler-world and attribute-syntax materialization.

`builtInBindingCommandExpressionType(...)` exposes the product-free parser entry family for a built-in command value. Use it when source lowerers or previews need to validate generated `.bind`, `.trigger`, `repeat.for`, `.state`, or `.dispatch` values without duplicating command-build policy.

@aurelia/state command parsing is framework-shaped but asymmetrical: `.state` uses the function/listener entry family because the renderer parses the state binding source as `IsFunction`, while `.dispatch` is a state command whose renderer parses the action source as `IsProperty`.

`BuiltInBindingCommandName` is the enum-backed command-name authority for framework-owned binding commands. Use it when app-builder or other source-producing code needs to mention built-in command names outside a concrete command instance.

`builtInBindingCommandAttributeText(...)` is the product-free source serializer for generated binding-command attributes. Keep `target.command` formatting there so source producers and parser round-trip validation share the same built-in syntax substrate.

`inlineMultiBindingValueSourceText(...)` is the product-free source serializer for inline custom-attribute multi-binding values. Keep generated `route: ...; params.bind: ...` segment formatting next to `parseInlineMultiBindingSegments(...)` so source producers do not invent a second multi-binding grammar.

`authored-template-source.ts` owns product-free authored-template attribute, text-content, element, and child-node source serialization. Use it for generated `name="value"` attributes, static attributes, plain visible text, structured template elements, mixed text/element children, and attribute append operations before adding app-builder-local source string helpers.

`binding-expression-source.ts` owns product-free authored binding-expression fragments for interpolation, iterator headers, binding behaviors, and value converters. Use it when generated source needs those forms instead of interpolating `${...}`, `item of items`, `expr & behavior`, or `expr | converter` locally.

anchors.summary: Materializes framework-owned syntax catalog products.
Selects syntax catalogs admitted by framework registrations.
Built-in syntax catalog data model and package/group entries.
Product-free built-in attribute parser used by source-lowering integrity checks.
Product-free command value parser-family helper for generated/source-lowering command values.
Enum-backed command-name authority for framework-owned binding commands.
Product-free source serializer for generated framework-owned binding-command attributes.
Product-free source serializer for inline custom-attribute multi-binding values.
Product-free source serializer for authored template attributes.
Product-free source serializer for plain authored template text content.
Product-free source serializer for structured authored template elements.
Product-free source model for mixed text and element children under authored template elements.
Product-free source serializer for repeat-like iterator binding headers.
Product-free source serializer for binding-behavior modifier expressions.
Inventory helper for built-in attribute-pattern definitions before configured compiler-world selection.

records.summary: TemplateCompilationProjectPass is the front-door project pass that wires compiler-worlds, template parsing/lowering, compiled-template assembly, authoring templates, recursive child-view context, and runtime analysis.
records.rationale: Large fixture or authoring work often needs the whole template pipeline rather than one local materializer. This pass is the durable place to understand phase order, authoring-template options, runtime-analysis depth, project-level compiled-template context, and the route from app-world resources to checker-backed runtime products.
guidance: Start here when work touches full template compilation, authoring template compilation, runtime-analysis depth, or phase profiling.
Use child materializer records for local behavior, but keep project-pass orchestration in this front-door pass.

TemplateRuntimeAnalysisProjectContext carries compiled-template emissions so runtime rendering and scope construction can relate parent usage sites to child custom-element definitions without re-running the compiler front door.

The project pass owns one CheckerExpressionTypeWorld for app and authoring resource runtime analysis, while each TemplateRuntimeAnalysisFrame records expression-cache deltas from a marker. Keep runtime/checker lifetime work here instead of recreating the world per resource.

Captured `...$attrs` work should keep the parent usage scope through dynamic instruction context claims: syntax origin, captured hydration instruction, and captured runtime controller. The scope is the hydration-context controller scope's parent, matching framework SpreadBinding.bind. Do not rely on syntax-only provenance or fall back to definition-wide captured attribute buckets.

Use opt-in fine-grained runtime-analysis phase telemetry when optimizing this frontier. Pair SEMANTIC_RUNTIME_TELEMETRY_FINE_PHASES=true with phase memory/kernel snapshots for density questions, or disable snapshots for cleaner CPU attribution.

This frontier is nextActionPolicy=when-touched after the aggregate child custom-element usage bridge closed; use it as front-door grounding, while runtime rendering/controller records own usage-local child view expansion.

Parent templates should hydrate child custom-element controllers, child view-model scopes, and aggregate child compiled-template views when renderer emulation created a child controller. This is required for wrapper/capture semantics such as field-shell `...$attrs`, because the child template's SpreadBinding must see the parent HydrateElementInstruction that captured the attributes.

Keep recursive component hydration finite with controller definition ancestry guards, not with definition-wide capture buckets or fresh-controller product handles. Built-in template-controller synthetic views remain recursive because their child instruction sequences are embedded in the same compiled template.

Recursive custom-element re-entry is now an intentional controller boundary, not an open seam: the child controller records `recursive-hydration-boundary` and API rows expose `childViewRenderingState=recursive-boundary`. Treat that as a finite aggregate analysis state; per-runtime-instance expansion and runtime-state termination policy are still future controller semantics.

Runtime analysis order is a project-pass invariant: schedule compiled resources by rendered-child dependency SCCs, using HydrateElementInstruction.definitionProductHandle and declared direct dependencies. Acyclic parents feed completed bound-controller facts to children; recursive groups share only predecessor facts so child overlays do not depend on incidental resource order.

Runtime-state-specific recursive expansion is not required for template overlays, ordinary TypeChecker diagnostics, or definition-level topology. Treat it as a prerequisite only when the inquiry asks for per-activation truth: SSR/SSG exact view trees for a route/data state, router/viewport activation reuse or deactivate ordering, dynamic composition lifecycle results, two-way/from-view feedback loops that change subsequent rendering, or stateful recursive component diagnostics.

Conditional template-controller branches need an explicit pass-through child scope even when TypeChecker narrowing produces no slots; otherwise nested branch work can clear the parent `if` marker before `else` link-hook claims are published.

The aggregate boundary is a memory/performance invariant, not just a recursion guard: a large-root canary dropped from roughly 388k records / 71k products / 404MiB construction heap to roughly 120k records / 23k products / 249MiB after child component internals stopped being duplicated under every parent usage site. If usage-local child views are needed, keep them aggregate and guarded rather than per-runtime-instance.

Controller observer setup diagnostics should stay demand-driven: only invoke ObserverLocator/TypeChecker support when a bindable has coercion or change-callback semantics to validate, and cache target-type property checks per definition.

Repeated option `model/value` bindings are a concrete recursive-rendering canary. The same-node `option repeat.for="..." value/model.bind="..."` case now closes through recursive rendering emission because the parent select owns the authored option node and the child synthetic view supplies the binding/scope; deeper cases where nested controllers produce descendant option nodes still need explicit rendered topology.
If a future inquiry only needs part of this pass, model that as inquiry depth/scope pressure instead of cloning a partial compiler pipeline.

anchors.summary: Template compilation project front-door pass.
Compiler-front-door emission shape for one resource.
Template runtime-analysis work frame.
Project-level compiled-template context used by recursive runtime/checker analysis.
Runtime Rendering emulation, including child custom-element aggregate render passes.

records.summary: TemplateCompilationUnitMaterializer owns the compiler-front-door products that parsing and lowering consume.
records.rationale: Large template work often needs source, parse context, root compilation context, and compilation-unit products before any HTML parse or instruction lowering can be understood. Keep this as a distinct phase from project-pass orchestration and child parser/lowering materializers.
guidance: Start here when a feature needs template source identity, source maps, recovery policy, parse frontier, owner metadata, or root compilation context.
Keep compilation-unit product construction distinct from project orchestration, HTML parse-tree materialization, and compiled-template assembly.
If new provenance or source-address pressure appears, improve this phase before adding local parser or renderer shortcuts.

anchors.summary: Template compilation-unit product materializer.
Claim publication companion for compilation-unit products.
Framework compiler phase grounding.

records.summary: HtmlParseMaterializer, HtmlParseTreeMaterializer, and HtmlScanner form the template HTML parse/recovery frontier.
records.rationale: Fixture and external-template pressure can expose complex but valid Aurelia markup. Parser gaps should deepen the HTML parse substrate and source-span model rather than creating feature-local scans or accepting broad carrier provenance.
guidance: Start here when template markup, recovery, element/text/comment spans, source maps, or malformed-looking-but-valid HTML/interpolation cases are involved.
Keep raw HTML scanning and parse-tree materialization distinct from attribute syntax parsing, expression parsing, and compiled-template assembly.
Treat broad source spans from this phase as accidental until exact provenance has been checked.

anchors.summary: Template HTML parse front door.
HTML parse tree materialization companion.
HTML scanner companion.
Framework compiler attribute-classification grounding after HTML parsing.

records.summary: BindingScopeMaterializer is the runtime binding-scope frontier for BindingContext, OverrideContext, Scope products, slot projection, and expression lookup.
records.rationale: Binding scope is a runtime/checker bridge, not compiler resource scope. Future template, controller, form, and LSP inquiries should preserve this layer when projecting viewmodel state, locals, and override-context semantics.
guidance: Start here when a feature needs runtime binding context, override context, scope products, or checker-facing slot projection.
Keep runtime binding scope distinct from compiler resource scope and template-controller flow scope.

Use BindingScope.lookup for reference-shaped expression consumers and BindingScope.locate when a materializer needs the concrete resolved Scope/context; do not reimplement parent/override/boundary traversal locally.

If view-model fields disappear from Aurelia expression lookup, inspect BindingScopeSlotProjector and lazy CheckerType member projection before patching downstream router, observation, completion, or diagnostic materializers.
If a new inquiry needs partial scope data, expose scope depth or projection controls instead of re-materializing local scope objects.

anchors.summary: Runtime binding scope materializer.
Runtime binding scope vs compiler resource scope notes.
Concrete Scope lookup result for materializers that need the resolved modeled Scope/context.

records.summary: RuntimeControllerBindMaterializer is the bind-phase frontier between controller semantics, runtime bindings, and binding-scope effects.
records.rationale: Hydration is already being emulated; the open question is recursive/runtime-dependent controller and binding consequences. This class should stay grounded in Aurelia controller/binding phase boundaries instead of generic small-class splits.
guidance: Inspect controller bind materialization before changing binding setup, scope effects, bind-time target operations, or lifecycle-adjacent products.
Use framework.rendering and framework.lifecycle to decide whether behavior belongs to creation, hydration, binding, or activation.
Split only along Aurelia phase/interface boundaries when size becomes a problem.

anchors.summary: Runtime controller bind materializer.
Bind-phase publication companion.
Framework binding setup rows.
Framework lifecycle relationship rows.

records.summary: Template pipeline work should follow the compiler-world to compiled-template to renderer/controller path before adding local feature products.
records.rationale: Aurelia already has a rich compiler/rendering/controller pipeline. Future semantic-runtime work should reuse that product path instead of inventing isolated template feature logic when a symptom appears in fixtures or LSP-like inquiries.
guidance: Start with compiler-world, compilation-unit, compiled-template, runtime-rendering, view-factory, and controller-bind materializers as a chain.
Use framework.compiler for instruction production and framework.rendering for renderer/controller consumption.
If the product question only needs one phase, expose that phase as an inquiry depth/scope rather than rebuilding the whole chain locally.

anchors.summary: Compiler-world start of the product chain.
Runtime rendering handoff.
Controller bind handoff.

records.summary: RuntimeValueConverterMaterializer is the invocation frontier for value-converter applications after rendered bindings and resource scope exist.
records.rationale: Value-converter behavior is runtime invocation pressure, not generic expression syntax. Keeping this materializer tracked prevents future framework-error work from rediscovering `sanitize` or routing converter issues through API-local diagnostics.
guidance: Start here when work touches built-in or custom value-converter invocation effects, converter-owned diagnostics, or expression ASTs that contain `ValueConverterExpression` nodes.
Walk interpolation hole expressions when collecting value-converter applications: runtime-html binds and evaluates each InterpolationPartBinding expression, not only the outer Interpolation AST.
Source value-converter application and issue products to the exact converter name span when the binding carrier has an admitted source file; broad binding-carrier spans should be fallback only.
Keep missing converter resource lookup in the runtime-html AST/binding-utils lane; this materializer owns effects after the converter resource is visible.

Template overlays should call checker-visible converter `toView(...)` methods directly so overload order and argument diagnostics come from TypeScript's native call resolution; the generated identity helper is only for missing converters or converter targets without `toView`, and dynamic `withContext` should emit both strict-true runtime branches. Keep the overlay branch projection paired with the direct TypeChecker expression evaluator union proof.

The first modeled framework path is `SanitizeValueConverter.toView(...)`: visible `sanitize` plus no modeled app `ISanitizer` resolver claims runtime-html `method_not_implemented` / `AUR0099`, while app `ISanitizer` registrations suppress it.
Before adding another converter-owned framework code, inspect `framework.errors` usage rows and use `framework.resources` / `framework.rendering` to confirm the owning runtime concept.

anchors.summary: Runtime value-converter materializer.
Semantic-runtime model of Aurelia's SanitizeValueConverter.toView path.
Framework-shaped value-converter semantics anchor.
Exact runtime-html AUR0099 authority for the sanitize path.

records.summary: BindingCommandLoweringMaterializer is the orchestration frontier for command-bearing attributes, inline multi-binding segment lowering, value sites, expression parses, and instruction emission.
records.rationale: The publisher record covers kernel publication, but the materializer owns the semantic flow from classified template attributes through parser/compiler-world services into runtime-shaped instructions. Its frame classes are intentional per-attribute and per-segment accumulators for lowering products, not API envelopes. Future template, forms, interpolation, or binding-command work should not treat them as untracked large classes or bypass them with feature-local lowering.
guidance: Start here when a template feature involves command-bearing attributes, multi-binding segments, command executable lookup, expression parses, or command-specific instruction emission.

Use MultiBindingSegmentLoweringFrame for secondary inline custom-attribute segment accumulation; do not add parallel arrays in the materializer when a segment-local product needs records, claims, issues, value sites, parses, or open seams.
Keep product publication concerns in BindingCommandLoweringPublisher and orchestration/data-flow concerns in BindingCommandLoweringMaterializer unless the framework suggests a sharper split.

Empty command values infer source expressions from the authored target before DOM target-property mapping; `implicit-binding-expression-inference` preserves cases such as `minlength.bind` flowing from `minlength` into target `minLength`.
Cross-check framework.compiler and framework.rendering before adding a new binding-command product or target operation.

anchors.summary: Binding command lowering orchestration class.
Publication companion that owns product envelopes and kernel records.
Binding-command lowering work frame.
Inline multi-binding segment lowering work frame.
Binding-command execution context frame.

anchors.scenario: empty binding command value source inference
anchors.summary: Pressure fixture for empty `.bind`, `.two-way`, and `.from-view` values where authored source inference stays distinct from target-property mapping.

records.summary: BindingCommandLoweringPublisher concentrates binding-command product publication and kernel record pressure.
records.rationale: Binding command lowering is a high-risk historical failure area. Publication pressure here should be followed through framework compiler/rendering/observation surfaces before introducing new product records.
guidance: Inspect binding-command lowering before adding new binding-command products or provenance records.
Treat repeated KernelStoreRecord construction pressure as a possible publication abstraction signal.
Cross-check framework.compiler instruction products and framework.rendering binding-products before product changes.

anchors.summary: Binding command lowering publication class.
Binding-command product publication companion.
Kernel record construction pressure in this module.

records.summary: Use the existing evaluator and TypeChecker expression substrates before creating feature-local static evaluation or branch-local lookup logic.
records.rationale: Compaction drift has repeatedly made future work forget whether module-scoped evaluation, TypeChecker expression evaluation, and template-local speculative context are separate. This record is a reusable starting handle.
guidance: Global module/import/static closure work starts in StaticEvaluator and project evaluation.
Template or lifecycle-local expression facts start in CheckerExpressionTypeEvaluator, TemplateScopeTypeProjector, and the modeled Scope products.
Expression-local branch facts now enter through CheckerExpressionScopeNarrower plus speculativeBindingScopeOverlay; reuse that path before adding inquiry-local autocomplete narrowing.
If you need both global module state and branch-local template facts, design a layered speculative context that shares module state instead of cloning evaluator setup.

anchors.summary: Global static ECMAScript evaluator.
TypeChecker-backed expression evaluator.
Uncommitted same-level Scope overlay for TypeChecker branch speculation.
Template scope type projection adjacent to local speculative contexts.
Product symbol coupling around static evaluation.

records.summary: Recursive rendering and control-flow work should start from controller, renderer, view-factory, template-controller, binding, and observer concepts already modeled.
records.rationale: The project has a recurring risk of inventing non-Aurelia reconciliation patterns. This record keeps future work grounded in modeled Aurelia runtime concepts before broadening fixture or LSP behavior.
guidance: Start with runtime-controller, runtime-rendering, runtime-view-factory, and template-controller flow materializers.
Use framework.rendering, framework.lifecycle, and framework.observation as shape checks before modeling new runtime consequences.

Generated authoring recipes can now verify built-in template-controller and synthetic-view hydration through row-backed runtime-controller expected effects; promise helpers assert branch-to-owner links for `pending`/`then`/`catch`, and switch helpers assert case-to-switch links for `case`/`default-case`. Use those as pressure canaries before adding source-level fixture shortcuts.

Compiled instruction products are definition-level, while runtime controller frames are instance-level; recursive rendering can produce several controllers for one instruction, so scope construction and branch-link publication must use the active parent controller context.
If recursive hydration is blocked by runtime-dependent viewmodel state, model controller semantics rather than pretending the branch is closed.

anchors.summary: Controller semantics model.
Rendering materializer.
Template-controller flow scope materializer.
Framework rendering hydration-flow surface.
Framework observation graph.

records.summary: TemplateControllerScopeMaterializer is a live pressure point for nested template-controller scopes.
records.rationale: Recursive rendering and control-flow typing are becoming central product pressures. Scope materialization should stay aligned with controller semantics, built-in template controllers, and TypeChecker-backed viewmodel state.
guidance: Read this before adding local if/repeat/switch scope handling.
Use framework.rendering and framework.lifecycle to check whether a flow belongs to controller creation, bind, hydration, or lifecycle semantics.
Do not treat nested template controller scopes as plain lexical TypeScript scopes; they sit between runtime controller semantics and checker projection.

Repeat/iterator scope construction now has an explicit `IteratorScopeMaterializationFrame`: it owns the source-value evaluator, rendered-binding source projector, iterator type projection, local type map, and static local handoff before issues, local slots, override slots, and BindingScope publication are assembled.

Let binding static values now pass through `LetStaticValueEvaluationFrame`: it keeps parse recovery, runtime binding lookup, source-expression context projection, and source-value evaluator handoff together before the slot receives either a concrete value or an explicit binding-scope boundary.

anchors.summary: Template-controller scope materializer.
Repeat/iterator scope materialization frame for source-value, source-expression, type projection, and local-slot handoff facts.
Repeat/iterator scope setup phase before issue publication, local-slot construction, and BindingScope preparation.
Repeat local-slot construction phase that attaches TypeChecker-projected local types and representative static values.
Let binding static-value frame for parse recovery, runtime binding lookup, source-expression context projection, and evaluator handoff.
Let binding source-value setup phase before evaluating or publishing a binding-scope boundary value.
Let binding source-value consumer that spends the projected context through RuntimeBindingSourceValueEvaluator.
Template-controller flow scope materializer.
Template-scope construction work frame.
Framework hydration-flow grounding.

records.summary: RuntimeRenderingMaterializer is the main pressure point for renderers, controller creation, view factories, and recursive hydration consequences.
records.rationale: The user clarified that hydration is already being emulated, but recursive hydration is gated by runtime-dependent viewmodel state. This materializer should be kept honest against framework rendering phases.
guidance: Check runtime-view-factory and runtime-controller materializers before adding another rendering handoff.
Use framework.rendering relationships to decide whether a behavior belongs in renderer dispatch, hydration, binding setup, or controller creation.

Root render analysis should mirror AppRoot's child-container boundary, and controller creation should register CustomElement/CustomAttribute local resource dependencies into the controller container before nested renderer lookup.

When custom-element rendering reaches a repeated definition through controller ancestry, record a controller `recursive-hydration-boundary` lifecycle step and surface `childViewRenderingState=recursive-boundary`; do not publish an open seam merely because the aggregate model refuses unbounded runtime expansion.

Do not reopen runtime-state-specific recursive expansion for overlay/typechecking-only questions. Reopen it when a consumer needs a concrete activation path or exact rendered tree, especially SSR/SSG, router/viewport lifecycle ordering, dynamic composition lifecycle, stateful recursive data components, or binding feedback loops that change later rendering.
Use `contract:recursive-rendering` when changing recursive child-view rendering, synthetic-view scope handoff, parent-to-child bindable flow, or getter observed-dependency provenance.

Controller-activation diagnostics that scan view-model class bodies should anchor spans through the checker declaration's already-admitted source-file address via the type-system declaration-source bridge. Do not borrow the resource target span; imported/factory-backed targets can point at a different authored site than the class body.
If the class grows, split along Aurelia phase/interface boundaries rather than invented product categories.

anchors.summary: Runtime rendering materializer.
Adjacent view-factory materializer.
Adjacent controller-creation materializer.
Adjacent controller-publication materializer.
Runtime rendering emission carrier.
Focused recursive custom-element, synthetic-view, bindable-flow, and getter-provenance contract.
Self-recursive custom-element pressure fixture for finite aggregate rendering boundaries.
Runtime renderer invocation frame.
Template rendering run frame.

records.summary: RuntimeControllerFrame intentionally concentrates mutable controller state, child controllers, bindings, lifecycle steps, and scope readiness.
records.rationale: The Aurelia framework controller is itself a large runtime shape. The semantic-runtime frame should stay close to that concept and split only when framework phase/interface boundaries make the split clearer than the single controller frame.
guidance: Inspect this before splitting controller semantics or moving binding, child, scope, or lifecycle state into a feature-local object.
Use runtime-controller creation, publication, and bind materializers for product emission; keep the frame focused on in-progress controller state.
If size becomes the pressure, split along Aurelia creation, hydration, binding, activation, or lifecycle-readiness boundaries rather than generic small-class categories.

anchors.summary: Mutable semantic-runtime controller frame.
Framework controller creation/hydration grounding.
Framework controller lifecycle grounding.

records.summary: RuntimeViewFactoryMaterializer owns the Rendering.getViewFactory/IViewFactory lane for embedded template-controller views.
records.rationale: Recursive rendering pressure should not hide view-factory semantics inside generic rendering code. Aurelia creates IViewFactory values for embedded views, and semantic-runtime needs that lane to remain explicit for controller trees, synthetic views, and SSR/SSG-facing products.
guidance: Start here when template controllers, embedded definitions, synthetic views, or IViewFactory products are involved.
Keep view-factory product publication distinct from renderer dispatch and controller bind effects.
Use framework.rendering render-consequences before changing view-factory claims or synthetic-view controller creation.

anchors.summary: Runtime IViewFactory product materializer.
Framework rendering consequences including view-factory-related flow.

records.summary: RuntimeSpreadBindingCreator and RuntimeTemplateCompilerSpreadCompileHost are the runtime SpreadBinding.create / TemplateCompiler.compileSpread handoff for captured attributes.
records.rationale: Spread/captured-attribute behavior sits between runtime rendering and compiler semantics. It should stay modeled as a SpreadBinding.create-shaped hydration-context walk plus a compiler handoff with explicit value-site and instruction products rather than being flattened into renderer-local special cases.
guidance: Start here when spread attributes, ...$attrs, captured binding commands, dynamic instructions, dynamic value sites, or spread hydration-context lookup are involved.
Check framework compiler/rendering relationships before changing spread instruction lowering or product ownership.
Keep RuntimeSpreadBindingCreator focused on the SpreadBinding.create side: controller parent walk, captured AttrSyntax lookup, and TemplateCompiler.compileSpread admission.

Model ...$attrs transfer through runtime hydration-context/controller parent flow; definition-wide capture fallbacks merge unrelated component instances and should be treated as a modeling regression.

Treat unsupported spread cases as semantic-runtime compiler-host gaps before adding local renderer shortcuts, except for source-proved renderer-dispatch failures such as SpreadValueRenderer invalid targets, which belong in RuntimeRendererIssue before any binding exists.

Dynamic instructions produced by compileSpread should carry direct captured-attribute context instruction and context controller claims. Scope construction should spend those exact claims and then use the context controller scope's parent as the dynamic instruction scope, matching framework SpreadBinding.bind. If the exact controller or parent scope does not resolve, leave the dynamic instruction unscoped rather than falling back to root scope or syntax-only captured-attribute buckets, because repeated wrapper definitions under different template-controller scopes can otherwise merge.

Downstream value-channel, data-flow, i18n, composition, and bound-controller value evaluation should resolve dynamic spread-created binding sources through the binding render context and RuntimeInstructionScopeLookup, not by choosing the first scope for a reused instruction product.

anchors.summary: Semantic SpreadBinding.create counterpart.
Runtime compileSpread host.
Compiler-world interface for spread compilation.
Framework compiler flow to compare before spread-host changes.

records.summary: RuntimeRenderedInstructionRecorder owns renderer-output publication into runtime bindings, target operations, scope effects, and claims.
records.rationale: Renderer dispatch produces several runtime products whose ownership can become muddy during fixture-driven expansion. This recorder should stay the publication boundary for rendered instruction consequences rather than scattering binding and target-operation records through individual renderers.
guidance: Start here when renderer output needs runtime binding, target operation, scope effect, render-context, or rendered-instruction claim publication.
Keep renderer-specific creation in runtime-renderer classes and product publication in this recorder unless framework.rendering suggests a sharper boundary.
Use framework.rendering binding-products and binding-admissions before changing rendered binding ownership.

anchors.summary: Rendered instruction consequence recorder.
Framework rendering binding product grounding.

records.summary: BuiltInRuntimeRendererCatalogMaterializer and its configured companion are the framework-owned runtime renderer catalog frontier.
records.rationale: Runtime renderer availability is a compiler-world input with framework registration and configuration pressure. Keep catalog product materialization explicit instead of hiding renderer visibility inside generic runtime-rendering dispatch.
guidance: Start here when a feature changes built-in renderer availability, package renderer catalogs, configured renderer selections, or compiler-world renderer visibility.
Keep raw runtime renderer catalog materialization distinct from configured catalog selection, mirroring the built-in syntax and resource catalog pattern.
Use framework.rendering and framework.admission before deciding renderer visibility belongs inside an individual renderer.

anchors.summary: Materializes framework-owned runtime renderer catalogs.
Selects runtime renderer catalogs admitted by framework registrations.
Runtime renderer model and default renderer catalog declarations.
Framework renderer and syntax-product grounding.

records.summary: TemplateAuthoringCompilerWorldMaterializer constructs standalone RuntimeHtml compiler worlds for authoring-time component analysis.
records.rationale: Authoring and LSP-like inquiries need a compiler world even when a project-local component is not yet attached to a modeled Aurelia.app root. This is a deliberate authoring lane, not a replacement for app-root hydration.
guidance: Start here when authoring, resource-library, or cursor-locus work needs compiler-visible built-ins without an app root.
Keep the standalone authoring compiler world separate from hydrated app compiler worlds.
TemplateCompilationProjectPass profiles this lane as authoring-compiler-world, separate from app component-compiler-world materialization.
Cursor-locus facade fallback should select the cursor source file by default, then widen only through explicit authoring source lists or authoringTemplateLimit.

This frontier is nextActionPolicy=when-touched: keep it as grounding for resource-library/cursor-locus authoring templates, not as default autonomous work when the next pressure is authoring verification or taste ontology.
If this lane grows, check whether the missing concept belongs in app-world construction, resource recognition, or inquiry depth rather than cloning compiler-world construction.

anchors.summary: Authoring-time RuntimeHtml compiler-world materializer.
Shared compiler-world construction lane.
Resource definitions that authoring worlds expose as local compiler-visible resources.

records.summary: TemplateValueSiteMaterializer and TemplateValueSitePublisher own compiler-selected template value sites and parser-owned expression products.
records.rationale: Class/style interpolation, binding commands, text interpolation, and command-specific values all converge at template value sites. Future fixture or LSP pressure should deepen this materialization/publication lane before inventing local expression extraction.
guidance: Start here when work touches text interpolation, attribute value ownership, binding-command values, expression parses, or value-site provenance.
Keep value-site selection in the materializer and product/kernel publication in the publisher unless framework compiler grounding suggests a sharper split.
Use the interpolation parser and binding-command lowering records together when value-site pressure involves expression parsing.

Publish durable value-site products only for values that enter parser, command, compiler, or runtime ownership. Static text and plain platform attributes should stay on HTML/syntax products unless a query-local value-site claim is explicitly needed.

anchors.summary: Template value-site selection materializer.
Template value-site product publication companion.
Framework attribute and value classification grounding.

records.summary: AttributeSyntaxMaterializer, AttributeClassificationMaterializer, and AttributeParserService own runtime-shaped attribute syntax and classification before value-site lowering.
records.rationale: Class/style binding pressure and complex interpolation cases depend on correct attribute syntax parsing and classification. This lane should stay framework-compiler shaped instead of relying on ad hoc attribute-name heuristics.
guidance: Start here when work touches attribute commands, attribute patterns, syntax parsing, bindable classification, custom attributes, or class/style binding forms.
Check framework.compiler attribute-classification rows before changing syntax or classification semantics.
Keep syntax parsing, classification, and value-site materialization as adjacent but distinct phases.

anchors.summary: Attribute syntax parse materializer.
Attribute classification materializer.
Runtime-shaped attribute parser service.
Framework compiler attribute classification grounding.

records.summary: TemplateScopeTypeProjector is the checker-backed bridge from controller/binding scopes to template-visible TypeScript symbols.
records.rationale: Recursive rendering, template-controller flow scopes, weak app typings, and LSP-like cursor loci all need a reusable projection of scope slots into checker-visible shapes. This should not be rediscovered inside individual inquiries.
guidance: Start here when template scope, local symbol, binding-pattern local, or checker-visible template member projection changes.
Keep TypeChecker shape publication in CheckerTypeProjector and scope-specific projection in TemplateScopeTypeProjector.
If a cursor-locus or diagnostic cannot explain a template symbol, inspect scope construction before adding inquiry-local lookup logic.

anchors.summary: Template scope TypeChecker projection service.
Runtime binding scope materializer that supplies scope products.
Product symbol coupling around template scope type projection.

records.summary: Interpolation parsing is a live substrate frontier because Aurelia supports multiple interpolation holes and template expressions inside holes.
records.rationale: The user clarified that apparent nested interpolation from external app sampling can be valid Aurelia syntax. This record keeps future parser work grounded in that behavior instead of treating those cases as malformed input.
guidance: Do not classify multiple interpolation holes or template expressions inside interpolation as malformed without checking Aurelia parser semantics.
If interpolation mode accepts hole expressions until EOF, reassess whether the parser needs stronger hole-boundary lookahead.
Keep interpolation provenance exact: broad carrier/header spans are accidental until proven intentional.

anchors.summary: Interpolation parser.
Interpolation publication frame.
Framework compiler flow for expression/parser grounding.

records.summary: RuntimeBindingBehaviorMaterializer owns bind-time binding-behavior application and issue products after renderer/controller bind facts exist.
records.rationale: Binding-behavior diagnostics are not generic parser or API wording checks. They need rendered RuntimeBinding products, compiler resource scope, controller bind target access facts, and framework-grounded binding behavior semantics before spending runtime-html or validation-html error authority.
guidance: Start here when built-in binding-behavior effects, duplicate rate-limit/target-subscriber diagnostics, signal/update-trigger/self/attr/validate semantics, or binding behavior application products change.
Keep expression parsing and behavior expression extraction below bindingBehaviorExpressions and runtimeAcceptedBindingExpressionAstForParse; do not reparse expression text in app/API diagnostics.

Walk interpolation hole expressions when collecting binding-behavior applications: runtime-html binds each InterpolationPartBinding expression and lets behaviors such as & state call useScope(...) for that hole.
Source binding-behavior application and issue products to the exact behavior name span when the binding carrier has an admitted source file; broad binding-carrier spans should be fallback only.
Keep bind-time target facts flowing through RuntimeControllerBindEmission and RuntimeBindingBehaviorBindEffectReader before publishing framework-error authority.

Binding-mode behaviors are resource-visible bind-time effects, not parser aliases: share the effective mode through runtime-binding-mode-behavior.ts so controller bind target access, value-converter phases, bound-controller values, template-controller source assignment, and observation data-flow do not grow separate mode interpreters or let unresolved behavior syntax mutate direction.

ValidationHtmlConfiguration admits ValidateBindingBehavior as a resource; validate-specific AUR4200-AUR4205 diagnostics must stay gated on resource visibility and closed parser-owned facts. AUR4206 is a malformed/foreign-AST fallback and should stay unclaimed until semantic-runtime admits that input surface.
Use appendRuntimeBindingProductValue for emission binding indexes shared with value-converter materialization.

anchors.summary: Materializes binding-behavior applications and issues after controller bind.
Validation-html validate binding-behavior semantics.
Reads framework-shaped bind effects for built-in binding behaviors.
Shared binding-product index append helper for binding-behavior and value-converter emissions.
Runtime-html binding and binding-behavior error-code grounding.
Validation-html validate and ValidationController error-code grounding.

records.summary: RuntimeCompositionMaterializer owns dynamic AuCompose CompositionContext and CompositionController products after bind/data-flow facts exist.
records.rationale: Static AuCompose setter/name failures are controller-input diagnostics, but dynamic composition is a runtime composition branch. It needs binding source evaluation, hydration-context container lookup, TypeChecker constructable candidate projection, and API pressure rows so dynamic component candidates do not look closed merely because no open seam was emitted.
guidance: Start here when work touches `<au-compose>`, CompositionContext, CompositionController, component/model binding evaluation, or dynamic composition candidate reporting.

Keep static invalid `scopeBehavior`, `flushMode`, and missing static string component diagnostics in the controller issue lane; dynamic component/model resolution belongs to runtime composition after controller bind and binding data-flow.
Use TypeChecker constructable candidates as a semantic fallback when value evaluation stays open but the component binding source type names custom-element classes.

Resource-definition lookup can enter through module-local identity when evaluator values carry a module key, or through unique local declaration identity when a TypeChecker declaration is the carrier.

Runtime composition rows now include the first lifecycle handoff: absent/parameterless `activate` closes as such, and `activate(model)` compares the model binding source type with the activation parameter type through shared checker assignability. The checker-backed activation handoff lives in `runtime-composition-activation.ts`; keep it reusable for future routeable-component activation comparisons rather than burying it in the composition scanner.

`activate(model)` parameter projection spends the shared `checker-signature-parameters.ts` overload candidate-basis. Do not regress runtime composition to `getCallSignatures()[0]`; overloaded lifecycle hooks should compare the model against all arity-compatible first-parameter candidates through checker-backed union projection.

AuCompose activation analysis now has explicit method and parameter frames: `activateMethodProjection(...)` owns member discovery, declaration location, value type, and callable signature admission; `activationParameterProjectionFrame(...)` owns candidate-basis and parameter-reference projection; `activationModelHandoffForParameterProjection(...)` owns absent/model-open/assignability handoff folding.

Runtime composition rows now report candidate resource-analysis coverage and candidate resource-controller counts by joining resolved component definitions back to their project-level template/runtime analyses.

Runtime composition contexts distinguish absent, direct, promise-fulfilled, and open component/template/model inputs so generated fixtures can verify promise-valued `AuCompose` support without inferring it from component resolution alone.
Closed static/value custom-element composition branches now materialize an aggregate composed child controller; TypeChecker-only candidate unions remain candidates rather than fake child controllers.

Closed static/value custom-element child-controller materialization now enters through `ComposedChildControllerMaterializationFrame`: admission owns parent container and resolved definition, then the materialization phase owns child-container emission, runtime controller construction, lifecycle step, host-child claim, and controller publication.

Parent-to-child component/model closure depends on `RuntimeBoundControllerValueTable` being threaded into child resource analysis and extended with the current resource's rendered controller values during runtime composition. Treat `Array.find receiver did not reduce to a known array` on bound child controller state as binding-source/evaluator pressure before adding AuCompose-local shortcuts.

Broad `Constructable` child composition can still close when the bound controller value table gives the child getter a concrete parent value and the evaluator binds receiver `this` for property method calls such as `entry.isApplicable(id)`. Exact evaluated object literals should read absent properties as `undefined`, not unknown shape.

`RuntimeCompositions` exposes `renderingContextKind` so definition-local resource analysis can be separated from recursive parent-supplied child instances. A public component's own template may honestly remain open when a required bindable is consumer-supplied even if all observed app use-sites close.

Do not treat current candidate resolution, candidate resource-analysis coverage, or aggregate composed child-controller handoff as full recursive composition rendering. Child scope handoff, lifecycle run/deactivate errors, template-only runtime template compilation, and per-branch recursive rendering remain controller/composition lifecycle frontiers.

Runtime-state-specific recursive expansion becomes a prerequisite only when dynamic composition asks for concrete lifecycle/run/deactivate consequences or exact per-activation rendered children. Candidate resolution, activation model type comparison, and aggregate child-controller rows should stay finite.

Keep `RuntimeCompositions` and `pressure:app-api` buckets in sync so composition candidates, compiled-template coverage, candidate resource-analysis coverage, input fulfillment, composed child-controller counts, activation handoff kinds, and open composition rows stay visible in aggregate app pressure.

anchors.summary: Materializes runtime AuCompose composition contexts and controllers.
Runtime composition relation-claim boundary linking host controller, composition context, and resolved component definitions.
Closed AuCompose custom-element child handoff frame for parent container admission, resolved definition, child-container emission, controller creation, and publication side effects.
Admission boundary for a closed static/value AuCompose candidate before materializing the aggregate child controller.
Finite aggregate materialization phase for composed child container, runtime controller, lifecycle step, relation claim, and controller publication.
Runtime composition controller product model with auLink grounding.
Checker-backed AuCompose activate(model) lifecycle handoff analysis.
AuCompose activate member discovery and callable signature admission.
Overload candidate and parameter-reference projection frame for activate(model).
Activation model handoff fold over parameter projection and model assignability.
API projection for runtime composition rows.
Bound controller value table consumed by child binding-source evaluation and runtime composition.
Static evaluator call semantics, including receiver-bound property method calls.

anchors.scenario: TypeChecker-visible AuCompose dynamic component candidates and bound child controller value flow
anchors.summary: Pressure fixture where repeat-local and child-bound values carry constructable component inputs into `component.bind`.
Framework composition grounding lane.
Framework hydration/rendering handoff for dynamic composition and controller ownership.

records.summary: Repeat-local value carriers bridge Aurelia Repeat BindingContext semantics into checker-backed template source evaluation.
records.rationale: A repeat local can have a useful static representative value even when the recursive rendered view is not materialized per item. Future router, binding, and authoring work should reuse the scope materializer and binding-source evaluator handoff instead of patching feature-local static values.
guidance: Start from TemplateControllerScopeMaterializer when repeat.for locals need value-side semantics; it owns the BindingContextSlot creation and can attach representative static values.

Use `iteratorScopeMaterializationFrame(...)` and `iteratorLocalSlots(...)` as the current materializer-side repeat scope boundary: the frame prepares source-value and source-expression context once, while local slots spend the iterator projection plus `repeatStaticLocalValue(...)`.
Use repeatStaticLocalValue to project conservative representative values from statically known iterables, including common string-pattern property values.

repeatStaticLocalValue should enter source-value reduction through projectRuntimeBindingSourceValueContextInScope(...), including its no-runtime-binding fallback, so repeat locals do not re-own binding-behavior lifecycle, strict-mode, or value-converter resource-scope policy.

RuntimeBindingSourceValueEvaluator should consume BindingContextSlot.staticValue before falling back to TypeChecker member access; local object/property evaluation belongs there rather than in router or authoring code.

This pass closes the binding-source-slot-no-static-value pressure for static repeat arrays, but it is not recursive per-view hydration. Treat per-item child rendering and controller-bind-value timing as separate future substrate work.

anchors.summary: Projects representative value-side Repeat local carriers from static iterable sources.
Constructs template-controller scopes and assigns repeat local static values.
Repeat scope setup boundary shared by local type projection, static values, and source-expression context handoff.
Repeat local slot construction that spends iterator local types and representative static values.
Runtime binding-context slot now carries optional staticValue.
Binding source evaluator consumes slot static values and local property access.
Pressure fixture where a repeat local object feeds router href/value-source evaluation.
Pressure harness that showed binding-source-slot-no-static-value disappeared after the pass.
Work Router query that should route repeat-value work into template recursive-rendering semantics.

records.summary: Built-in runtime-html and ui-virtualization template-controller flow now share one framework-resource-backed overlay contract surface.
records.rationale: The built-in controller fixture covers if/else, repeat, with, portal, promise/pending/then/catch, and switch/case/default-case through runtime controller rows, durable BindingScope narrowing, and generated overlay type inference, including repeat array/map/set/number source categories, strict-literal, typeof, property-presence, and instanceof if/else narrowing, plus switch/case discriminant replay for scalar cases, array cases, default exclusions, and static fall-through chains. The focused contract compares runtime-html template-controller semantics with the runtime-html built-in resource catalog and the framework-source-derived target/name table. It also admits @aurelia/ui-virtualization DefaultVirtualizationConfiguration, materializes the virtual-repeat resource catalog row, proves frameworkTemplateControllerSemantics includes virtual-repeat, and uses a dedicated fixture to prove virtual-repeat.for item locals and $index flow through the same overlay path. Future work should extend the framework-shaped semantics, scope materializer, narrower, and overlay prelude owners rather than adding answer-local TypeScript fallbacks or fixture-only patches.
guidance: Start with template-controller-semantics.ts when a runtime-html template-controller needs a value property, flow kind, or child-view cardinality.

Use TemplateControllerFlowScopeMaterializer for child Scope effects; with.bind value scopes should project the non-nullish object binding context before BindingScopeSlotProjector derives visible members.

Use TemplateTypeSystemOverlayBuilder and the overlay prelude for checker-visible scope replay; promise then/catch target expressions are owned by promise-result scope declarations, not standalone expression probes.

Repeat overlay replay should keep array, map, set, and numeric repeat source categories in the shared __au_repeat helper and prove them through the built-in template-controller contract before adding local iterable shortcuts.

Switch/case replay should use the authored switch expression, read static case values from SetPropertyInstruction as runtime strings, read bound expression values from expression products, and model blocking previous cases plus static fall-through chains before narrowing a case branch.
Default-case replay should exclude all readable direct case values; unreadable previous cases or dynamic fall-through should degrade the branch to a plain block instead of inventing a predicate.

Durable BindingScope slot narrowing for if/else strict literal equality, typeof primitive guards, property-presence `in` guards, `instanceof` constructor guards, switch equality, and fall-through belongs in CheckerExpressionScopeNarrower and TemplateControllerFlowScopeMaterializer; overlay replay should spend those scope facts rather than duplicating API answer code.

Run pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/semantic-runtime contract:template-controller-built-ins after changing built-in template-controller semantics, scope replay, resource catalog mirrors, framework-registration admission, or overlay helper generation.

Use pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/atlas framework:resources -- --projection=convergence --resourceKind=template-controller --detail --rows=30 when deciding whether a template-controller coverage claim is runtime-html-only or broader framework/plugin pressure.

Treat virtual-repeat template-controller resource/scope/overlay flow as covered by this contract, but keep ui-virtualization CollectionStrategyLocator, IDomRenderer, scroller, DOM renderer, and plugin error-policy semantics as a separate deeper pressure frontier.

anchors.summary: Framework template-controller semantic catalog including runtime-html controllers and virtual-repeat.
Framework-shaped child Scope construction for built-in template-controller flow.
Named overlay helper inventory for generated TypeScript surfaces.
Generated TypeScript overlay builder that replays materialized BindingScope ancestry.
Pressure fixture exercising runtime-html built-in template-controller families.
Runtime-html built-in resource catalog mirrored by the template-controller semantics contract.
UI virtualization resource catalog admitting the virtual-repeat template controller.
Pressure fixture proving virtual-repeat.for item locals and override-context overlay flow.
Contract for built-in template-controller runtime rows and overlay type inference.
Framework resource convergence rows used to separate runtime-html template-controller coverage from plugin controller pressure.

records.summary: Template TypeScript overlay expression projection has an explicit AST-kind support matrix, generated-child splice path, and owned prelude helper inventory.
records.rationale: Overlay expression projection sits between Aurelia expression parsing and TypeScript checker overlays. It should not rediscover ownership from raw syntax. The support matrix names which AST kinds are TypeScript-shaped, scope-alias-backed, resource-lowered, transparent wrappers, owner-handled, statement-frontier, or opaque owner surfaces. Generated child expressions can now be spliced into TypeScript-shaped parent source while preserving authored source segments. The prelude table contains only reusable emitted helper declarations; promise-result locals, scope aliases, and temporary locals belong to overlay layer planning rather than empty helper rows. Treat template-controller semantics, binding/value flow, observer flow, checker projection, and framework resource admission as overlay substrate, not adjacent feature work: overlays should spend those products instead of rebuilding local TypeScript-only approximations.
guidance: Start with template-type-system-overlay-expression-support.ts before adding or removing an UnsupportedSyntax lane.

The old unsupportedOverlayExpressionSyntax helper was retired; if that symbol still appears as the next work item, treat it as Atlas/Work Router hygiene and route to the support matrix rather than rebuilding a blacklist.

Keep the overlay thread centered even when the immediate work is in template controllers, bindings, observers, resources, or checker projection. Those layers are dependencies that must be connected before overlays can stay faithful without split-brain.

Keep value-converter syntax lowered through RuntimeValueConverterMaterializer, direct checker-visible `toView(...)` calls, and the overlay prelude identity fallback; do not treat value converters as arbitrary JavaScript child expressions in authored templates. Dynamic checker-visible `withContext` must model both runtime strict-true branches rather than choosing one arity locally, and the generated overlay should agree with CheckerExpressionTypeEvaluator on the branch return union.

Copied source expressions must consume the runtime binding projection's `strictBinding` axis. Strict-false read/call sites lower to optional-chain-shaped TypeScript so overlay diagnostics agree with Aurelia non-strict nullish `astEvaluate`; assignment targets opt out because writeability and assignability belong to binding data-flow/source assignment policy.
Keep binding behaviors value-transparent in expression overlays; bind-time behavior effects and diagnostics belong to RuntimeBindingBehaviorMaterializer.
Keep ForOfStatement, Interpolation, BindingIdentifier, and binding patterns owner-handled. Repeat scope construction and interpolation hole binding should project their child value expressions.
Treat CustomExpression as an owner surface, currently i18n TranslationBinding, rather than as generic unsupported TypeScript syntax.

Treat DestructuringAssignment as statement-overlay pressure only if the semantic parser admits it. At the current checkpoint, semantic-runtime normalizes repeat destructuring into binding patterns; Atlas expression:coverage reports that class as normalized-unconstructed with dormant statement-frontier flags rather than an active overlay gap.
TypeScript-shaped parents may splice generated child expression parts only when spans are source-backed and non-overlapping; preserve parent source segments for diagnostic mapping.

Add named TypeScript helper declarations in template-type-system-overlay-prelude.ts rather than inlining helper declarations at expression projection sites. Each helper row should name an owner, emitted declaration names, and non-empty declaration text.

The repeat prelude helper should use type-level item projection instead of an `any` fallback overload: preserve `any` as `any`, but let `unknown` sources stay unknown so TS18046 can surface through public overlay diagnostics.

Repeat unknown preservation is not overlay-only: checker repeat element projection and template scope construction should carry an explicit unknown slot so cursor/member diagnostics report no projected members rather than missing-slot-type.

Repeat locals produced by synthetic array methods depend on hydrated related type references. If Array.flat/flatMap or nested array literals lose a slot type, inspect CheckerTypeShapeAccess.iteratedValueType before adding overlay-local helpers.
Do not add empty prelude rows for concepts that emit no reusable helper. Promise result locals, $this/$parent aliases, and generated temporary locals are overlay plan/layer facts.

Keep TemplateTypeSystemOverlaySkippedReason as an emitted-fact vocabulary, not a planning wishlist. Add a skip reason only when the builder can actually emit it and the owner/substrate gap is understood.

Run pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/semantic-runtime contract:type-system-overlays after changing expression support, prelude helpers, value-converter lowering, scope alias replay, or overlay source segment construction.

anchors.summary: Support and ownership matrix for every semantic-runtime expression AST kind.
Expression projector that copies source, lowers resource-backed expressions, and splices generated children into TypeScript-shaped parents.
Named helper declarations available to generated template TypeScript overlays.
Low-level source text used by the generated-child splice contract.
Atlas coverage probe for parser construction, support-matrix ownership, and overlay/evaluator consumer rows for expression AST kinds.
Contract for overlay source mapping, support matrix coverage, generated-child splicing, value-converter lowering, aliases, diagnostics, and public overlay rows.

records.summary: UI virtualization now has template-controller resource/scope/overlay coverage plus configuration-owned service resolver effects, while DOM renderer, scroller, collection strategy, and error-policy runtime semantics remain open.
records.rationale: DefaultVirtualizationConfiguration registers CollectionStrategyLocator, DefaultDomRenderer, and VirtualRepeat. Semantic-runtime recognizes the configuration, admits the virtual-repeat resource catalog row, models virtual-repeat as repeat-like iteration for template scope and TypeScript overlay flow, and spends the CollectionStrategyLocator/IDomRenderer service registrations as framework resolver effects. That closes the template-controller and DI service surface, but not the plugin's DOM renderer/scroller substrate, collection strategy policy, or framework error-code diagnostics.
guidance: Start with this frontier when external app pressure or framework error-code pressure involves @aurelia/ui-virtualization beyond ordinary virtual-repeat item-scope typing.

Do not add local virtual-repeat fixture hacks: resource admission belongs in FrameworkRegistrationKind/Capability plus UiVirtualizationBuiltInResourceCatalogs, and item-scope semantics belong in frameworkTemplateControllerSemantics.

Treat scroller lookup, horizontal/table policy, item sizing, near-top/near-bottom events, and collection-strategy runtime behavior as deeper runtime/plugin semantics, not as template-controller scope facts.

Do not reopen DefaultVirtualizationConfiguration merely because CollectionStrategyLocator and DefaultDomRenderer are services: semantic-runtime spends those as framework resolver effects; further work should target their runtime behavior and diagnostics.

Use Atlas framework error and DI lenses against the ui-virtualization package before adding diagnostics for virtualization errors; the current template-controller contract does not prove renderer/scroller/strategy error authority.

anchors.summary: Framework registry that admits CollectionStrategyLocator, DefaultDomRenderer, and VirtualRepeat.
Framework virtual-repeat template-controller, collection observation, scroller, sizing, and event behavior.
Semantic-runtime capability that admits the virtual-repeat resource header.
Semantic-runtime resource catalog for virtual-repeat.
Repeat-like template scope/value-flow semantics for virtual-repeat.
Synthetic fixture proving virtual-repeat.for item local and override-context overlay flow.
Contract that verifies runtime-html template controllers plus virtual-repeat plugin controller coverage.
Use the ui-virtualization package rows when choosing virtualization diagnostic/error-code work.

records.summary: Template overlays are usable but not complete; the remaining hardening is integration work across semantic products, checker overlays, diagnostics, provenance, rename readiness, and split-brain guards.
records.rationale: Generated TypeScript overlays now have a Program-owned source path, expression support matrix, prelude inventory, template-controller scope replay, explicit overlay diagnostics, and authored-source segment mapping. The open frontier is not to make overlays locally cleverer. It is to ensure overlays spend the same semantic-runtime products as diagnostics, binding/data-flow, observer value channels, i18n CustomExpression owners, evaluation/value-source readers, and future LSP edit plans. The risk is split-brain: TypeScript may accept generated overlay code while Aurelia binding/writeback semantics reject it, a semantic diagnostic may already own the same authored span, or a future rename may see only a generated segment without the non-overlay Aurelia reference family.
guidance: Start here when asking whether overlays are complete for diagnostics, typechecking, rename, MCP, or LSP readiness.

Template overlays consume source-value facts indirectly through materialized BindingScope slots and bound-controller/runtime-composition products. Do not make generated TypeScript call RuntimeBindingSourceValueEvaluator locally; fix the upstream binding-flow/source-value path if a slot or value fact is missing.

Use retired-symbol queries such as unsupportedOverlayExpressionSyntax as route hygiene canaries: they should point at the overlay expression support matrix and integration route, not at fresh evaluator/parser implementation work.

Keep template-controller, binding/data-flow, observer, i18n, router, resource, and evaluator work framed as overlay substrate when the overlay needs those facts; do not add TypeScript-only overlay approximations. Overlay-local `$this`/`$parent` state should stay in the alias replay cursor, while scope facts still come from materialized BindingScope products.

When overlay replay projects a source expression that creates a child scope, use the expression's runtime source scope rather than the final leaf scope. Repeat iterable sources, let sources, with.bind values, promise sources, and switch/case values should see the parent/source scope aliases that Aurelia would have used at evaluation time. Same-level template-controller conditions can read prior same-level scope effects that have already been replayed into the aggregate synthetic scope, but runtime scope effects must not read the local they are creating.

Runtime-binding overlay source reads should enter through RuntimeBindingSourceExpressionContextProjector rather than a behavior-name precheck. For source-scope-changing binding behaviors, wrap the copied source expression instead of mutating the generated child-view scope. `& state` changes the binding source evaluation scope through framework binding.useScope(...), while a template-controller child view such as repeat still keeps the original view-model parent for `$parent`.

The current copied-source wrapper only synthesizes `StateBinding` replay tails because `& state` is the only modeled bind-time source-scope-changing behavior. If another framework binding behavior calls binding.useScope(...), extend the runtime source-expression projector and shared overlay layer vocabulary first; do not add an overlay-local fallback for arbitrary BindingScopeOwnerKind values.

Runtime-binding overlay source reads also carry strictBinding. Strict-false read/call expressions should generate TypeScript that models non-strict nullish `undefined` results rather than surfacing TS18047 from copied text, while assignment targets stay on the binding data-flow/source-assignment lane.

Cursor/member-owner reads that need a runtime binding source projection for an expression offset should use bindingSourceContextProjectionForTemplateExpressionParseAtOffset(...) from template-expression-selection.ts, not a completion-local RuntimeBindingSourceExpressionContextProjector fork.

When cursor completion already has the selected ambient BindingScope, pass it into bindingSourceContextProjectionForTemplateExpressionParseAtOffset(...). That lets repeated controller/rendering applications disambiguate runtime bindings through templateScopeCanEvaluateSourceScope(...) before falling back to a raw known-scope checker context.

If an overlay source-expression projection produces a source scope unrelated to the ambient overlay scope, keep it as explicit unsupported pressure. Do not recast the projected expression through ambient aliases; the proper fix is lower scope/threading coherence, not a generated-TypeScript fallback.

Runtime binding selection for overlays should be scoped by `templateScopeCanEvaluateSourceScope(...)`. Same-level synthetic scopes may evaluate a source scope only when `templateScopeCanReplaySourceScope(...)` proves the ambient synthetic scope replayed the source creators and visible slots; a shared parent alone is not sufficient. Identity/source-backed slot type differences are valid refinements, but anonymous source-less slots must keep the same projected type before replay can treat them as the same fact.

Plural runtime binding source-context selection must converge explicitly. If candidate bindings project to different source scopes, strictness, binding-behavior lifecycle modes, source addresses, or expression spans, the shared selector should stay open rather than allowing overlays, cursor diagnostics, completions, or future edit surfaces to pick the first compatible runtime binding by emission order.

`templateScopeSourceReplayRelation(...)` owns the broader generated-analysis question: can an ambient scope copy this source expression directly, or must it synthesize deeper source-scope replay layers? Keep that relation in template-scope-replay rather than re-growing overlay-local ancestry checks.
